Description:
As a User, I want to register a new account with email and password, so that I can start using the app.

AC-01: The system must display a registration form with fields: Full Name, Email, Password, Confirm Password.
AC-02: When the user submits the form, the system must validate all required fields are filled.
AC-03: If Email is already registered, the system must display: "Email already exists."
AC-04: If Password does not match Confirm Password, the system must display: "Passwords do not match."
AC-05: If Password is less than 6 characters, the system must display: "Password must be at least 6 characters."
AC-06: On successful registration, the system must create the account, log the user in automatically, and redirect to the Home screen.
AC-07: The system must return access token and refresh token for session management.

Summary: AUTH-02: Send OTP to Email - BE
Description:
As a User, I want to receive an OTP code to my email, so that I can verify my email address.

AC-01: The system must display a "Send OTP" button after entering email.
AC-02: When the user clicks "Send OTP", the system must generate a 6-digit OTP code.
AC-03: The system must send the OTP to the user's email within 30 seconds.
AC-04: The OTP must expire after 5 minutes.
AC-05: If the email is invalid, the system must display: "Invalid email format."
AC-06: On success, the system must display: "OTP sent to your email."
AC-07: The user can request a new OTP after 60 seconds (resend cooldown).

As a User, I want to verify my email by entering the OTP code, so that my account becomes verified.

AC-01: The system must display an OTP input screen with 6 digit boxes.
AC-02: When the user enters the correct OTP, the system must mark the account as verified.
AC-03: If the OTP is incorrect, the system must display: "Invalid OTP code."
AC-04: If the OTP has expired, the system must display: "OTP expired. Please request a new one."
AC-05: After 5 failed attempts, the system must block further attempts for 15 minutes.
AC-06: On successful verification, the system must redirect to the Home screen.

As a User, I want to login with my email and password, so that I can access my account.

AC-01: The system must display a login form with Email and Password fields.
AC-02: When the user submits the form, the system must validate credentials.
AC-03: If the email is not registered, the system must display: "Email not found."
AC-04: If the password is incorrect, the system must display: "Invalid email or password."
AC-05: On successful login, the system must redirect to the Home screen.
AC-06: The system must return access token (expires in 1 hour) and refresh token (expires in 7 days).

As a User, I want to login with my Google account, so that I can sign in quickly without creating a new password.

AC-01: The system must display a "Continue with Google" button on the login screen.
AC-02: When the user clicks the button, the system must redirect to Google OAuth consent screen.
AC-03: After Google authentication, if email already exists (local account), the system must link the Google account to the existing account.
AC-04: If email does not exist, the system must create a new account with Google information.
AC-05: On success, the system must redirect to the Home screen with the user logged in.
AC-06: The system must store Google Subject ID for future logins.

As a User, I want my session to be refreshed automatically, so that I don't have to login again frequently.

AC-01: When the access token expires, the system must use the refresh token to obtain a new access token.
AC-02: If the refresh token is valid, the system must return a new access token.
AC-03: If the refresh token is expired or invalid, the system must redirect to the Login screen.
AC-04: The system must not require user interaction for token refresh.

As a User, I want to update my profile information (name, bio, avatar), so that I can personalize my account.

AC-01: The system must display a Profile Edit screen with current user information pre-filled.
AC-02: The user can edit: Full Name, Bio (max 500 characters), Avatar.
AC-03: When the user saves changes, the system must update the profile.
AC-04: On success, the system must display: "Profile updated successfully."
AC-05: If Bio exceeds 500 characters, the system must display: "Bio must be under 500 characters."

As a User, I want to create a new Routine, so that I can start organizing my daily habits.

AC-01: The system must display a "Create Routine" form with fields: Title, Description, Category, Theme Color, Schedule, Visibility.
AC-02: Title is required. If empty, the system must display: "Title is required."
AC-03: If Title exceeds 200 characters, the system must display: "Title must be under 200 characters."
AC-04: The user can select Category from: Health, Study, Work, Lifestyle, Other.
AC-05: The user can select Schedule: Daily (every day) or Weekly (specific days).
AC-06: The user can set Visibility: Private or Public.
AC-07: On save, the system must create the Routine and redirect to the Routine Detail screen.
AC-08: The system must display: "Routine created successfully."

Summary: RTN-02: Add Tasks to Routine - BE
Description:
As a User, I want to add Tasks to my Routine, so that I can break down my habits into actionable steps.

AC-01: On the Routine Detail screen, the system must display an "Add Task" button.
AC-02: When adding a task, the user must enter: Task Title, Task Type (Checkbox or Quantity), Icon.
AC-03: If Task Type is Quantity, the user must enter: Target Value (e.g., 2000) and Unit Name (e.g., "ml").
AC-04: The user can select an icon from the icon library for visual appeal.
AC-05: Tasks must be displayed in the order they were added.
AC-06: On save, the system must add the task and display: "Task added."

As a User, I want to reorder my Tasks by dragging, so that I can prioritize the most important steps first.

AC-01: The system must allow drag and drop to reorder tasks on the Routine Detail screen.
AC-02: When the user releases a task in a new position, the system must save the new order.
AC-03: The new order must persist when the screen is refreshed.
AC-04: On success, the system must display a brief confirmation (e.g., toast: "Order updated").

As a User, I want to edit my Routine details, so that I can update the title, description, or schedule.

AC-01: The system must display an "Edit" button on the Routine Detail screen.
AC-02: When clicked, the system must open the Edit form with current values pre-filled.
AC-03: The user can modify: Title, Description, Category, Theme Color, Schedule, Visibility.
AC-04: On save, the system must update the Routine and display: "Routine updated successfully."
AC-05: Only the owner of the Routine can edit it.

As a User, I want to delete my Routine, so that I can remove habits I no longer follow.

AC-01: The system must display a "Delete" button on the Routine Detail or Edit screen.
AC-02: When clicked, the system must display a confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to delete this Routine?" with "Yes" and "No" options.
AC-03: If the user clicks "Yes", the system must delete the Routine and redirect to the My Routines screen.
AC-04: The system must display: "Routine deleted successfully."
AC-05: If the user clicks "No", the system must close the dialog with no changes.
AC-06: Only the owner of the Routine can delete it.

As a User, I want to view all my Routines with their Tasks, so that I can see my habits at a glance.

AC-01: The system must display a "My Routines" screen listing all Routines owned by the user.
AC-02: Each Routine card must show: Title, Theme Color, Category, Task Count.
AC-03: When the user taps a Routine, the system must navigate to the Routine Detail screen.
AC-04: If the user has no Routines, the system must display: "No routines yet. Create your first one!"
AC-05: Deleted Routines must not appear in the list.

As a User, I want to set my Routine to repeat on specific days of the week, so that I can customize when to perform my habits.

AC-01: When creating/editing a Routine, the user can select "Weekly" schedule.
AC-02: If Weekly is selected, the system must display day checkboxes: Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun.
AC-03: The user must select at least one day. If none selected, display: "Please select at least one day."
AC-04: On the Home/Today screen, only Routines scheduled for today must be displayed.
AC-05: Daily Routines must appear every day.

As a User, I want to select an icon for each Task, so that my Routine looks visually appealing.

AC-01: When adding/editing a Task, the system must display an icon picker.
AC-02: The icon picker must show popular icons: water, book, run, sleep, eat, study, etc.
AC-03: The user can optionally select an icon color.
AC-04: If no icon is selected, the system must use a default icon.
AC-05: The selected icon must display next to the Task title.

As a User, I want to set my Routine as Private or Public, so that I can control who can see it.

AC-01: When creating/editing a Routine, the user can set Visibility: Private or Public.
AC-02: Private Routines are only visible to the owner.
AC-03: Public Routines appear in the Discover Feed and can be copied by others.
AC-04: Default visibility for new Routines must be Public.
AC-05: The visibility status must be indicated on the Routine card (e.g., lock icon for Private).

As a User, I want to tap a Checkbox task to mark it as done, so that I can track my daily progress.

AC-01: On the Today screen, each Checkbox task must display an unchecked circle.
AC-02: When the user taps the circle, the system must mark the task as Completed.
AC-03: The circle must change to a filled checkmark with animation.
AC-04: The Routine's completion percentage must update immediately.
AC-05: If the user taps again, the system must allow undo (uncheck).

As a User, I want to log a numeric value for Quantity tasks, so that I can track progress toward my target.

AC-01: On the Today screen, Quantity tasks must display: current value / target value (e.g., "500 / 2000 ml").
AC-02: When the user taps the task, the system must open a number input dialog.
AC-03: The user can add to the current value (e.g., add 250ml to existing 500ml).
AC-04: When current value reaches or exceeds target, the system must mark the task as Completed.
AC-05: A progress bar must visually show completion percentage.

As a User, I want to skip a task for today, so that I can mark it as intentionally not done without breaking my streak.

AC-01: Each task must have a "Skip" option (swipe or menu).
AC-02: When the user skips a task, the system must mark it as Skipped (gray color).
AC-03: Skipped tasks must not count toward the daily completion percentage.
AC-04: Skipped tasks must not break the user's streak.
AC-05: The user can "Unskip" to return the task to normal state.

As a User, I want to view my today's Routines with completion status, so that I can track my daily progress.

AC-01: The Today screen must display all Routines scheduled for today.
AC-02: Each Routine card must show: Title, completion percentage, and list of Tasks.
AC-03: Completed tasks must show a green checkmark.
AC-04: In-progress Quantity tasks must show a progress bar.
AC-05: An overall daily completion percentage must be displayed at the top.
AC-06: If all tasks are completed, the system must display: "Great job! All done for today!"

As a User, I want to undo my check-in for today, so that I can correct mistakes.

AC-01: Completed tasks must allow undo by tapping again.
AC-02: When undone, the task returns to "In Progress" state.
AC-03: The completion percentage must recalculate immediately.
AC-04: Only today's check-ins can be undone. Past days are locked.
AC-05: If user tries to edit past log, display: "Past logs cannot be edited."

As the System, I want to generate a daily summary at end of day, so that analytics can be calculated.

AC-01: At 23:59 (or user's timezone), the system must calculate and save the daily summary.
AC-02: The summary must include: total tasks scheduled, tasks completed, completion percentage.
AC-03: If completion percentage >= 80%, the day counts toward the streak.
AC-04: If the user had no scheduled tasks, no summary is generated.
AC-05: The job must run automatically without user action.

As the System, I want to calculate the user's current streak, so that users can see their consecutive days of success.

AC-01: Streak = number of consecutive days with completion rate >= 80%.
AC-02: A missed day (no activity or rate < 80%) resets the streak to 0.
AC-03: Skipped tasks do not count against the streak.
AC-04: The streak must update daily after the summary is generated.
AC-05: The system must also track "longest streak" for personal records.

As a User, I want to see my current streak on my profile, so that I feel motivated by my consistency.

AC-01: The profile screen must display: "X days streak" prominently.
AC-02: If streak is 0, display: "Start your streak today!"
AC-03: The longest streak must be shown below current streak.
AC-04: Streak must be visible on public profile as well.

As the System, I want to generate heatmap data from daily summaries, so that users can visualize their activity.

AC-01: The heatmap must show the last 90 days of activity.
AC-02: Each day cell has a color intensity based on completion rate: 0%=empty, 1-25%=light, 26-50%=medium, 51-75%=dark, 76-100%=full.
AC-03: Days with no activity must be shown as empty (gray).
AC-04: The user can view other periods: 30, 90, 180, or 365 days.

As a User, I want to see my activity heatmap on my profile, so that I can visualize my long-term consistency.

AC-01: The profile screen must display a GitHub-style heatmap grid.
AC-02: The grid must have 7 rows (days of week) and columns (weeks).
AC-03: Hovering/tapping a cell must show the date and completion rate.
AC-04: A legend must explain the color intensity levels.

As a User, I want to view another user's public profile, so that I can see their habits and progress.

AC-01: When navigating to a user's profile, the system must display: Avatar, Name, Bio, Streak, Heatmap, and public Routines.
AC-02: If the user has no public Routines, the system must display: "No public routines."
AC-03: The system must display follower count and following count.
AC-04: The system must show a Follow/Unfollow button (if not viewing own profile).

As a User, I want to upload a profile picture, so that my profile has a personal photo.

AC-01: The system must display an "Upload Photo" button on the Profile Edit screen.
AC-02: When the user selects a photo, the system must upload it to cloud storage.
AC-03: Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 5MB.
AC-04: If the file is too large, the system must display: "Image must be under 5MB."
AC-05: On success, the system must update the avatar preview immediately.

As a User, I want to upload a photo when checking in, so that I can share my progress with the community.

AC-01: When checking in a task, the system must display an optional "Add Photo" button.
AC-02: When clicked, the system must open the camera or photo gallery.
AC-03: Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 5MB.
AC-04: If file is too large, display: "Image must be under 5MB."
AC-05: On success, the photo must appear attached to the check-in.
AC-06: Photos must be stored securely in cloud storage.

Summary: PHOTO-02: View My Photo Gallery - BE
Description:
As a User, I want to view all my uploaded photos in a gallery, so that I can see my progress history.

AC-01: The Profile screen must have a "Gallery" tab showing all uploaded photos.
AC-02: Photos must be displayed in a grid, sorted by date (newest first).
AC-03: Tapping a photo must show full-screen view with: date, task name, routine name.
AC-04: If no photos, display: "No photos yet. Start capturing your progress!"

Summary: HEAT-03: View Other User's Heatmap - BE
Description:
As a User, I want to view another user's heatmap on their public profile, so that I can see their activity level.

AC-01: Public profiles must display the user's heatmap.
AC-02: Private profiles must hide the heatmap.
AC-03: The viewer cannot see detailed check-in logs, only the heatmap summary.

As a User, I want to copy a Routine from another user, so that I can follow their habit template.

AC-01: On a public Routine Detail screen, the system must display a "Copy" button.
AC-02: When clicked, the system must show confirmation: "Copy this Routine to your list?"
AC-03: If the user confirms, the system must clone the Routine (including all Tasks) to the user's account.
AC-04: The copied Routine must be set to Private by default.
AC-05: On success, redirect to the new Routine and display: "Routine copied successfully!"
AC-06: The copy count on the original Routine must increase by 1.

As a User, I want to edit my copied Routine independently, so that I can customize it for myself.

AC-01: A copied Routine must be fully editable (title, tasks, schedule).
AC-02: Changes to the copy must NOT affect the original Routine.
AC-03: Changes to the original must NOT affect the copy.
AC-04: The copied Routine must indicate it was copied: "Copied from @username".

As a Creator, I want to see how many people copied my Routine, so that I can measure its popularity.

AC-01: On my Routine Detail screen, the system must display: "X people copied this."
AC-02: The copy count must update in real-time when others copy.
AC-03: Optionally, the creator can view a list of users who copied (public profiles only).

As a User, I want to browse a Discover Feed of public Routines, so that I can find and copy routines from others.

AC-01: The Discover screen must display public Routines from all users.
AC-02: Each Routine card must show: Title, Category, Creator name & avatar, Copy count.
AC-03: The user can filter by Category (Health, Study, Work, etc.).
AC-04: The user can sort by: Most Popular (copies) or Newest.
AC-05: Tapping a Routine must navigate to the Routine Detail screen.
AC-06: Private Routines must not appear in the feed.

As a User, I want to see recent check-ins with photos from the community, so that I can get inspired.

AC-01: The Activity tab must display recent check-ins with photos.
AC-02: Each card must show: User avatar & name, Photo, Task & Routine name, Timestamp.
AC-03: Only check-ins from public Routines must be shown.
AC-04: The feed must be sorted by newest first.
AC-05: Tapping a card must navigate to the user's profile.

As a User, I want to search for Routines by keyword, so that I can find specific habits.

AC-01: The Discover screen must have a Search bar at the top.
AC-02: When the user types, the system must filter Routines matching the keyword.
AC-03: Search must match against Title and Description.
AC-04: If no results, display: "No routines found. Try a different keyword."
AC-05: Results must be displayed as the user types (live search).

As a User, I want to follow another user, so that I can see their activity in my feed.

AC-01: On a user's profile, the system must display a "Follow" button.
AC-02: When clicked, the system must add the user to my Following list.
AC-03: The button must change to "Following" (with option to Unfollow).
AC-04: If I try to follow myself, display: "You cannot follow yourself."
AC-05: The followed user's follower count must increase by 1.

As a User, I want to unfollow another user, so that I stop seeing their activity.

AC-01: On a followed user's profile, the button must show "Following".
AC-02: When clicked, display confirmation: "Unfollow @username?"
AC-03: If confirmed, remove them from my Following list.
AC-04: The button must revert to "Follow".
AC-05: The user's follower count must decrease by 1.

As a User, I want to view my followers and following lists, so that I can manage my connections.

AC-01: The Profile screen must display: "X Followers" and "X Following".
AC-02: Tapping either must open a list of users.
AC-03: Each user in the list must show: Avatar, Name, Follow/Following button.
AC-04: The list must be scrollable with pagination.

As a User, I want to see a feed of check-ins from people I follow, so that I stay connected with my network.

AC-01: The Activity tab must have a "Following" section.
AC-02: This section must show check-ins only from users I follow.
AC-03: Check-ins from private Routines of followed users must be visible (if I follow them).
AC-04: If I don't follow anyone, display: "Follow users to see their activity here."
